meta.title: Incident Investigation — Lost-Time Injury, Press Shop, Line 4
meta.subtitle: Plant 3, Maharashtra — de-identified; competent-person review required
meta.author: HSE Department
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before any analysis; the re-identification key is held separately, access-controlled, and is not part of this document. No injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published.

sections.text: On the incident date, Worker A sustained a lost-time hand injury at the Line-4 power press (Plant 3) when the fixed guard had been removed to clear a jam under production pressure. The single most important systemic cause is an organisational one: an ambiguous jam-clearance procedure combined with a lapsed refresher-training cycle that left guard-removal an accepted informal practice. Assessed against the Maharashtra accident-notice duty, the event is REPORTABLE — a state accident notice (Form 24) is due within 24 hours. This report is de-identified throughout; the re-identification key is held separately.

rows: Line 4 press jams on a mis-fed blank during the run.
Worker A removes the fixed guard to clear the jam (no isolation).
Press cycles on residual stored energy; Worker A's hand is struck.
Standby (W-1) raises the alarm; first aid administered.
Worker A account: jam, then guard removed to clear it.
Guard found detached at the scene; fixings loose.
Jam-clearance procedure does not state 'isolate before guard removal'.
Press controls show a cycle initiated during the clearance window.
Witness W-1 confirms guard was off and the line was under output pressure.
Training records: guard-safety refresher lapsed beyond its cycle for the crew.

sections.text: Method: ICAM (systems-based — chosen for a serious, high-potential event). The analysis runs the four ICAM categories and reaches the organisational layer; rca.validate(method='icam', analysis) returns reaches_systemic = true (>=1 Organisational Factor populated). The systemic factor is highlighted: the ambiguous procedure (E-3) and the lapsed training cycle (E-6) made informal guard removal an accepted practice under production pressure — not an isolated individual error.

rows: Fixed guard defeated; no isolation step enforced.
Guard removed to clear the jam without isolation.
Output pressure; jam-prone mis-feed on the run.
Ambiguous jam-clearance procedure; lapsed guard-safety refresher cycle.

rows.title: RC-1 (root)
rows.description: Organisational: the jam-clearance procedure omits an isolate-before-guard-removal step, and the guard-safety refresher cycle had lapsed — informal guard removal became accepted under output pressure.

rows.title: RC-2 (contributing)
rows.description: Engineering: the press allowed a cycle to initiate with the fixed guard removed (no guard-interlock).

rows.title: RC-3 (immediate)
rows.description: The guard was removed to clear a jam without isolating stored energy.

rows.action: Fit a guard-interlock so the press cannot cycle with the fixed guard removed (RC-2).
Rewrite the jam-clearance procedure to mandate isolation before any guard removal; brief the crew (RC-1).
Reinstate and complete the lapsed guard-safety refresher for the press crew (RC-1).

rows.control: Guard-interlock so the press cannot cycle with the guard removed
Reduce mis-feed jams at source (feed-alignment fix) to remove the clearance task
Isolate-before-guard-removal mandated in the rewritten procedure

items: ISO 45001 clause 10.2 (incident, nonconformity & corrective action) — the management-system basis for this investigation and its CAPA.
Maharashtra — REPORTABLE: the state accident-notice form (Form 24, within 24h) resolved via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS with the state confirmed; never a national form number.
OSH-Code transition note: the accident-notice duty is retained under the OSH Code; Maharashtra has not yet notified — file under the legacy state form.
Re-identification key held separately; no injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published in this circulated report.
